name: Cloudfuze
summary: Cloud migration platform for moving files, chats, emails, and tenant data between connected storage providers and SaaS environments.

account.benefits: An account is required to start a free trial, add source and destination clouds, and run migration jobs.

developer.notes: Developer and automation support have not yet been verified from first-party sources.

allowed_file_types.notes: Supported file types likely depend on the connected providers, but that still needs first-party verification.

security.notes: Official FAQ says CloudFuze uses OAuth to access connected clouds, does not store cloud passwords, and is SOC 2 compliant.

sources.label: CloudFuze homepage
sources.notes: Official homepage describing CloudFuze as an enterprise migration and SaaS-governance platform.

sources.label: CloudFuze FAQ
sources.notes: Official FAQ covering free-trial limits, account creation, plan-dependent migration size, provider throttling, and security behavior.

sources.label: CloudFuze pricing
sources.notes: Official pricing page describes quote/demo-oriented paid migration licensing rather than a durable free plan.

workflow_modes.notes: CloudFuze markets migrations for files, chats, emails, and tenant data, alongside backup and sync-style workflows.

execution_model.notes: Current CloudFuze pages describe plan-based migration jobs between connected providers and SaaS environments.

item_limit.notes: Conditional: CloudFuze says transfer size depends on the active plan and trial type. Free trials are limited, while larger migrations can scale to terabytes or petabytes.

included_storage.notes: Conditional: CloudFuze manages migrations between connected providers and does not market a standalone personal storage quota on the first-party pages reviewed.

scheduled_runs.notes: Current first-party sources reviewed here do not clearly publish a dedicated scheduling model.

provider_dependency.notes: CloudFuze runs migrations between connected providers rather than exposing a standalone end-user storage bucket.

bandwidth_model.notes: Conditional: CloudFuze says transfer speed depends on data size, folder structure, and vendor throttling, and that migrations halt when allowed data-transfer quota is exhausted.

reason: Rejected for the free-first dataset after current official review: CloudFuze is a paid migration service with only a limited trial/demo path, not a durable practical free tier.

name: CloudHQ
summary: Cloud sync, backup, and migration service for keeping data moving between connected cloud apps and storage providers.

account.benefits: An account is required to set up sync, backup, migration, or cloud-to-cloud workflows.

developer.notes: Developer and automation support have not yet been verified from first-party sources.

allowed_file_types.notes: Supported file types likely depend on the connected providers, but that still needs first-party verification.

security.notes: The reviewed first-party pages confirm HTTPS product surfaces, but they do not position cloudHQ as end-to-end encrypted cloud storage.

sources.label: cloudHQ homepage
sources.notes: Official homepage for cloudHQ.

sources.label: cloudHQ file size upload limits
sources.notes: Official support article stating that provider limits vary and that the cloudHQ free plan upload limit is 150 MB.

sources.label: cloudHQ getting started sync
sources.notes: Official getting-started page describing background sync and backup workflows between cloud apps.

sources.label: cloudHQ sync wizard page
sources.notes: Official sync wizard page showing one-way and two-way cloud sync setup.

workflow_modes.notes: Current official pages position cloudHQ around sync, backup, and migration between cloud apps and storage providers.

execution_model.notes: Current cloudHQ pages describe background sync and backup flows between connected apps and storage providers.

item_limit.notes: Official cloudHQ support says the free plan upload limit is 150 MB, while underlying provider limits still vary by connected cloud service and account type.

included_storage.notes: Conditional: official pages position cloudHQ around sync and backup between cloud apps, not around a standalone personal storage quota.

scheduled_runs.notes: Official support describes unlimited sync and backup on the free plan, with plan-specific limits such as the 150 MB free transfer limit.

provider_dependency.notes: cloudHQ works across connected providers and background sync flows rather than through a standalone storage bucket.

bandwidth_model.notes: Conditional: official support says file-size and upload behavior vary by connected cloud app and account type, while cloudHQ itself works continuously in the background to sync or back up data.

name: Cloudsfer
summary: Cloud migration, backup, and sync service for transferring files between connected storage providers with quota-based plans.

account.benefits: An account is required to use trial quotas, create migrations, and run backup or sync jobs across providers.

developer.notes: Developer and automation support have not yet been verified from first-party sources.

allowed_file_types.notes: Supported file types likely depend on the connected providers, but that still needs first-party verification.

security.notes: The reviewed first-party pages confirm HTTPS product surfaces, but they do not position Cloudsfer as end-to-end encrypted storage.

sources.label: Cloudsfer homepage
sources.notes: Official homepage for cloud migration, backup, and sync workflows.

sources.label: Cloudsfer FAQ
sources.notes: Official FAQ covering provider-dependent file-size limits, trial behavior, and approximate transfer speed per job.

sources.label: Cloudsfer pricing
sources.notes: Official pricing page showing quota-based plans such as 25 GB/month, 100 GB/month, and 250 GB/month.

workflow_modes.notes: Current official pages position Cloudsfer around migrations, sync, and backup jobs across connected providers.

execution_model.notes: Official FAQ and pricing position Cloudsfer as a quota-based transfer and sync platform between connected clouds.

item_limit.notes: Conditional: official FAQ says file-size limits depend on the source and destination platform rather than on a universal Cloudsfer cap.

included_storage.notes: Conditional: official pages frame Cloudsfer around moving or syncing provider-owned storage rather than providing a separate personal storage bucket.

scheduled_runs.notes: Official FAQ and pricing describe scheduled, repeat, delta, sync, and backup workflows, with advanced scheduling depending on plan.

provider_dependency.notes: Cloudsfer migrates, syncs, and backs up provider-owned storage rather than providing its own personal cloud bucket.

bandwidth_model.notes: Conditional: official pricing gives the always-free/trial tier 250 MB of data volume and larger paid quotas, while the FAQ says throughput also depends on data size, file count, network speed, and platform limits.

name: Ctrlq
summary: Current ctrlq.org is a Digital Inspiration community forum about Google Workspace and Apps Script, not a cloud-migration service.

account.benefits: No distinct cloud-migration account workflow was verified; the current official site is a discussion community.

developer.notes: No public migration API or CLI is relevant for the current ctrlq.org forum surface.

allowed_file_types.notes: The current official site is a community forum, not a file-transfer or cloud-migration product.

security.notes: The current official forum is served over HTTPS, but it does not describe a migration-service security model.

sources.label: ctrlq.org homepage
sources.notes: Official current homepage showing Digital Inspiration community forum topics around Google Workspace and Apps Script rather than a cloud-migration service.

workflow_modes.notes: Current ctrlq.org is a community forum rather than a live cloud-migration product.

execution_model.notes: Current ctrlq.org does not expose a migration product execution model.

item_limit.notes: The current official site is a discussion forum, so there is no live cloud-migration file-size capability to preserve.

included_storage.notes: The current ctrlq.org surface is a community forum rather than a hosted migration or storage product.

scheduled_runs.notes: Current ctrlq.org is not a migration product.

provider_dependency.notes: Current ctrlq.org is not a migration or storage product.

bandwidth_model.notes: No current migration-transfer capability is exposed by the official ctrlq.org forum.

reason: Current ctrlq.org is a Google Workspace community forum, not a cloud-migration product.

name: FileStack
summary: Developer-focused file upload and content-ingestion platform with cloud-storage integrations, not a dedicated cloud-to-cloud migration manager.

account.benefits: An account is required to start the free trial, get an API key, and manage uploads, storage, transformations, and billing.

developer.notes: Official docs publish File, Management, Processing, Workflow, and SDK/API references for developers.

allowed_file_types.notes: Official marketing says Filestack can upload any file type, subject to application-side rules and Filestack security policies.

security.notes: Official pages emphasize secure transfer, security policies, domain whitelisting, and content scanning, but they do not position Filestack as end-to-end encrypted migration storage.

sources.label: Filestack pricing
sources.notes: Official pricing page listing storage, bandwidth, uploads, and max-file-size limits by plan.

sources.label: Filestack homepage
sources.notes: Official homepage positioning Filestack as a file uploader and upload API with cloud-storage integrations.

sources.label: Filestack docs
sources.notes: Official documentation hub covering APIs, SDKs, security, file upload, storage, and workflows.

workflow_modes.notes: Current Filestack product is a developer upload and content-ingestion platform rather than a cloud-to-cloud migration manager.

execution_model.notes: Current Filestack workflow is an API-driven upload/storage pipeline rather than a cloud migration runner.

item_limit.notes: Official pricing lists 1 GB max file size on the entry Start plan, with larger paid tiers increasing that cap.

included_storage.notes: Official pricing gives the Start plan 50 GB of Filestack-managed storage.

scheduled_runs.notes: Current Filestack product is not a scheduled cloud-to-cloud migration manager.

provider_dependency.notes: Filestack can use its managed storage or connected destinations, so storage behavior depends on product setup rather than on migration semantics.

bandwidth_model.notes: Official pricing gives the Start plan 75 GB of bandwidth.

reason: Current Filestack is a developer upload and content-ingestion platform rather than a dedicated cloud-to-cloud migration service.

name: Mover
summary: Retired Microsoft-acquired cloud migration service whose supported provider-to-Microsoft 365 flows were folded into Migration Manager.

account.benefits: No standalone Mover account onboarding remains for the retired service.

developer.notes: No current public API or CLI is relevant for the retired standalone Mover service.

allowed_file_types.notes: Historical migrations targeted supported Microsoft 365 scenarios, but the standalone Mover product is retired.

security.notes: Current first-party surface is Microsoft retirement and Migration Manager documentation, not a live standalone transfer product.

sources.label: Microsoft Learn: Migrate to Microsoft 365
sources.notes: Official Microsoft Learn page stating that Mover is retired, that supported source migrations are integrated into Migration Manager, and that individual/student use ended after October 31, 2024.

sources.label: Microsoft Learn: Migration Admin role
sources.notes: Official Microsoft Learn page describing current Migration Manager capabilities for Google Drive, Dropbox, Box, Egnyte, and file-share migrations.

workflow_modes.notes: Mover was a migration product historically, but Microsoft retired the standalone service and folded supported flows into Migration Manager.

execution_model.notes: Current Microsoft documentation says supported flows now live in Migration Manager instead of a standalone Mover surface.

item_limit.notes: Mover is retired for admin-led migrations, and individual/student use ended after October 31, 2024, so it no longer exposes a live standalone migration-size surface.

included_storage.notes: Mover does not offer a current standalone storage or staging service after retirement.

scheduled_runs.notes: The standalone Mover service is retired.

provider_dependency.notes: The standalone Mover service is retired.

bandwidth_model.notes: No current standalone transfer quota applies because the service has been retired and integrated into Migration Manager.

reason: Microsoft retired Mover and folded supported Google Drive, Box, Dropbox, and Egnyte migrations into Migration Manager.

name: NetDrive
summary: NetDrive and Bdrive CloudSync let users mount remote storage as local drives and run one-way sync tasks between local disks and cloud storages.

account.benefits: CloudSync requires a Bdrive account ID to purchase, log in, and run syncing. Personal plans allow one authenticated account per device, while team plans support multiple users.

developer.notes: No public API or CLI workflow was verified on current first-party NetDrive or CloudSync pages.

allowed_file_types.notes: Supported file types depend on the local or cloud storages being mounted or synchronized.

security.notes: Official docs expose HTTPS product surfaces and say CloudSync log files exclude credential information, but the product is not positioned as end-to-end encrypted storage.

sources.label: NetDrive homepage
sources.notes: Official homepage describing NetDrive as remote-storage mount software with background uploading and team-drive features.

sources.label: NetDrive pricing
sources.notes: Official pricing page showing free trials, personal versus team plans, and user-account limits.

sources.label: Bdrive CloudSync about
sources.notes: Official CloudSync overview stating that it synchronizes between storages automatically and that file transfer happens on the user's PC or Mac.

sources.label: Bdrive CloudSync synchronize manual
sources.notes: Official manual showing one-way synchronization behavior and task-based sync controls.

sources.label: Bdrive CloudSync authentication
sources.notes: Official authentication guide explaining that CloudSync requires a Bdrive account ID and documenting personal-plan device-login behavior.

sources.label: Bdrive CloudSync Dropbox config
sources.notes: Official task-configuration guide stating that users can control the bandwidth used from task options.

sources.label: Bdrive CloudSync settings
sources.notes: Official settings documentation stating that credential information is not included in CloudSync log files.

workflow_modes.notes: Current NetDrive pages position the product around mounted cloud drives and CloudSync workflows.

execution_model.notes: Current NetDrive product works as a desktop-mounted cloud-drive client with CloudSync support.

item_limit.notes: Conditional: official pages describe syncing between connected storages but do not publish one universal per-file cap for NetDrive or CloudSync tasks.

included_storage.notes: Conditional: NetDrive and CloudSync operate on linked local or cloud storages instead of marketing a separate hosted storage bucket.

scheduled_runs.notes: Current first-party sources reviewed here do not clearly publish a dedicated scheduling model.

provider_dependency.notes: NetDrive mounts and syncs linked cloud storage rather than selling a standalone storage bucket.

bandwidth_model.notes: Conditional: CloudSync lets users control bandwidth from task options, while file transfer happens on the user's PC or Mac.

reason: Rejected for the free-first dataset after current official review: NetDrive/CloudSync is free to try only, with paid licenses after the short trial.

name: Odrive
summary: Universal sync, sharing, backup, and CLI-capable client for linked cloud storage services, with current product messaging centered on Procore sync plus broader multi-storage documentation.

account.benefits: Accounts are required to start a trial, link storage, create auth keys, and use desktop, web, backup, or CLI workflows.

developer.notes: Official Sync Agent and CLI docs show scriptable desktop and headless workflows, but no separate public API docs were verified.

allowed_file_types.notes: Supported file types depend on the linked storage services being synced, shared, or backed up through odrive.

security.notes: Official security docs say odrive never stores user files, uses encrypted transport, encrypts stored authorization info, and offers premium zero-knowledge client-side encryption folders.

sources.label: Odrive Docs home
sources.notes: Official documentation home describing odrive as a universal sync client with sharing, backup, and storage-linking features.

sources.label: odrive pricing
sources.notes: Official pricing page showing free-trial access, plan structure, encrypted transfers, and the statement that there are no limits from odrive while linked Procore storage limits still apply.

sources.label: Odrive sync docs
sources.notes: Official sync documentation describing placeholder sync, reduced bandwidth usage, and reduced API call volume.

sources.label: Odrive security FAQs
sources.notes: Official security FAQ saying odrive does not store user files, uses encrypted transport, and encrypts stored authorization information.

sources.label: Odrive Sync Agent and CLI docs
sources.notes: Official Sync Agent and CLI documentation covering scriptable shell usage, auth keys, mount commands, and backup commands.

sources.label: Odrive encryption docs
sources.notes: Official documentation for premium zero-knowledge encryption folders with client-side encryption before files reach the cloud.

sources.label: odrive free-consumer offering announcement
sources.notes: Official announcement says odrive discontinued its free consumer offering effective March 31, 2026.

workflow_modes.notes: Current odrive pages position the product around universal sync, backup, sharing, and linked cloud storage access.

execution_model.notes: Current odrive docs describe desktop sync and a headless Sync Agent / CLI workflow.

item_limit.notes: Conditional: official pricing says there are no limits from odrive and that linked Procore storage limits apply normally.

included_storage.notes: Conditional: odrive never stores user files itself and pricing says there are no limits from odrive, so linked provider limits apply instead.

scheduled_runs.notes: Current first-party sources reviewed here do not clearly publish a dedicated scheduling model.

provider_dependency.notes: odrive links existing cloud storage providers and says its own limits do not override linked-provider storage limits.

bandwidth_model.notes: Conditional: sync docs say placeholder sync reduces unnecessary bandwidth usage and background API calls, so transfer behavior depends on what you sync and on linked services.

reason: Rejected for the free-first dataset after current official review: odrive discontinued its free consumer offering effective 2026-03-31, which is now in the past.

name: Otixo
summary: Otixo's current We.Team product combines team collaboration, integrated third-party cloud drives, and encrypted personal or team cloud storage.

account.benefits: Accounts are required to register with email, Google, or Apple and to access TeamSpaces, personal cloud storage, and connected external cloud drives.

developer.notes: No current public API or CLI workflow was verified on the official Otixo / We.Team pages reviewed.

allowed_file_types.notes: Supported file types depend on the personal We.Team Cloud, TeamSpace file sharing, and the connected third-party cloud drives.

security.notes: Official pricing says TeamSpace and personal We.Team Cloud storage are AES-256 encrypted, and the live product surfaces use HTTPS.

sources.label: Otixo / We.Team homepage
sources.notes: Official homepage showing the current We.Team product, free registration flows, multi-cloud integrations, and encrypted We.Team Cloud positioning.

sources.label: We.Team pricing
sources.notes: Official pricing page listing free and paid TeamSpace storage, personal cloud-drive storage, unlimited integrations, and AES-256 encryption claims.

workflow_modes.notes: Official We.Team pricing lists personal cloud drive storage, third-party cloud-drive integrations, and transfer of files/folders from third-party cloud drives to the personal cloud drive.

execution_model.value: Workspace with personal and connected clouds
execution_model.notes: The current Otixo / We.Team product combines workspace file sharing, personal We.Team Cloud Drive storage, and connected third-party cloud drives.

item_limit.notes: Conditional: official We.Team pages describe integrations across many cloud drives and protocols, but they do not publish one universal per-file cap.

included_storage.notes: Official pricing gives the free plan 5 GB of TeamSpace storage and 5 GB of personal We.Team Cloud Drive storage, while paid tiers raise TeamSpace storage per member.

scheduled_runs.notes: Current first-party sources reviewed here do not clearly publish a dedicated scheduling model.

provider_dependency.notes: Current Otixo / We.Team combines its own encrypted storage with connected third-party cloud drives.

bandwidth_model.notes: Conditional: official pages publish unlimited cloud integrations and meetings, but they do not give one standalone transfer-quota number for cloud-drive traffic.

name: OxygenCloud
summary: Legacy Oxygen Cloud branding now points to odrive product pages rather than a distinct standalone cloud-migration service.

account.benefits: No distinct standalone OxygenCloud account workflow remains separate from odrive's current product flows.

developer.notes: No separate public OxygenCloud API or CLI surface was verified on the current official pages.

allowed_file_types.notes: Current official pages describe the odrive storage-linking product rather than a distinct OxygenCloud file-policy surface.

security.notes: The current official Oxygen Cloud pages are HTTPS odrive pages and no longer describe a separate standalone OxygenCloud security model.

sources.label: Oxygen Cloud homepage surface
sources.notes: Official Oxygen Cloud-branded page now marketing odrive's unified sync, sharing, backup, and encryption product.

sources.label: Oxygen Cloud unified access page
sources.notes: Official page showing odrive branding, linked-storage capabilities, and the current business-focused discontinuation notice for free consumer offering.

workflow_modes.notes: Current Oxygen Cloud branding points at odrive pages rather than a distinct live migration service.

execution_model.notes: Current Oxygen Cloud pages resolve to odrive product surfaces rather than a distinct execution model.

item_limit.notes: Current Oxygen Cloud-branded pages are odrive product pages, so there is no distinct standalone OxygenCloud migration-size surface to preserve.

included_storage.notes: No distinct standalone OxygenCloud storage service is still being marketed on the current official pages.

scheduled_runs.notes: Current Oxygen Cloud branding points to odrive rather than a live standalone migration scheduler.

provider_dependency.notes: Current Oxygen Cloud pages are odrive-branded surfaces, not a standalone migration service.

bandwidth_model.notes: The current official Oxygen Cloud domain is effectively an odrive-branded marketing surface rather than a separate standalone migration tool.

name: Skyvia
summary: All-in-one cloud data platform for integration, backup, query, connect, and automation across cloud apps and databases, with plan-specific limits that vary by product.

account.benefits: The official site exposes sign-in and sign-up flows for app.skyvia.com, and the docs describe product-specific free and paid subscriptions under a user account.

developer.notes: The official pricing page and product navigation advertise developer-facing access methods such as OData, ODBC, ADO.NET, and MCP endpoints, but a single canonical API docs URL was not captured in this pass.

allowed_file_types.notes: Supported objects and file-like content depend on the connected providers and the specific Skyvia product in use rather than one global file-type policy.

security.notes: Official pages are served over HTTPS, and Skyvia Backup documentation says backed-up data are stored in secure Microsoft Azure cloud storage in encrypted form.

sources.label: Skyvia homepage
sources.notes: Official homepage describing Skyvia as an all-in-one cloud data platform with integration, backup, query, connect, and automation products.

sources.label: Skyvia pricing
sources.notes: Official pricing page listing per-product limits, including uploaded file size, records per month, backup storage, snapshot storing time, query traffic, and connect traffic.

sources.label: Skyvia Backup docs
sources.notes: Official documentation stating that backed-up data are stored in secure Microsoft Azure cloud storage in encrypted form and explaining backup product behavior.

sources.label: Skyvia subscription limits docs
sources.notes: Official documentation describing product-specific free-versus-paid limits, including free backup retention behavior and product-specific quotas.

workflow_modes.notes: Current Skyvia pages position the product around integration, backup, automation, query, and connect workflows.

execution_model.notes: Current Skyvia pages position the product as a hosted data platform running integration, backup, and automation jobs.

item_limit.notes: The official pricing page lists uploaded file size up to 400 MB for the data-integration plans.

included_storage.notes: The official pricing page gives the free Backup plan 1 GB of storage. Other Skyvia products use different limits, so service-wide storage is still product-specific.

scheduled_runs.notes: Current Skyvia positioning includes backup and automation workflows that run as account-managed jobs.

provider_dependency.notes: Current Skyvia limits vary by product, while data sources and connected systems still shape what can move.

bandwidth_model.notes: Conditional: Skyvia publishes different traffic and throughput limits by product. Examples include limited query traffic on the free Query plan, 100 KB/month on the free Connect plan, and product-specific records-per-month limits for integration.

reason: Rejected as out of scope for this file-hosting-oriented section: Skyvia is a database/cloud-app data integration and backup platform, not a practical file-host or cloud-drive migration tool for the directory.
